title: DataUpload
description: Normalized empirical data ready for Window 2 and audit comparison. Provenance is mandatory and load-bearing — every dataset must carry full attribution. Original files are never modified; all transformations are logged.
_extension_note: Top-level additionalProperties is true (resolved Q11, 2026-05-14): this is the designated extension surface — sessions add fields here per foundational-answers.md (tier, validation, declaration_trail, scalar_observables, source_filename), riding the same open-surface pattern as contract 01's `parameters` and contract 02's `*_state`. The required + named properties below are the stable, load-bearing contract; everything else is a documented extension. This schema is authoritative; the hand-rolled validators are a deliberate thin lagging subset (see foundational-answers.md §Q11).

upload_id.description: UUID. Used by AuditDelta to reference this dataset.

provenance.description: ATTRIBUTION IS MANDATORY. Window 2 and Window 3 MUST display this prominently. Every audit export carries it. This is the load-bearing 'dignity for data collectors' commitment.

license.description: e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0', 'public_domain', 'permission_granted', 'unknown'. NEVER null — if unknown, say so.

acknowledgments_text.description: Free-form acknowledgment text from the contributors. Displayed verbatim in Window 2 attribution panel.

bibtex.description: Pre-formatted BibTeX. Used in audit exports.

contact_email.description: For asking questions about the dataset.

version_or_doi_of_dataset.description: If the dataset itself has a separate DOI or version (e.g. Zenodo deposit).

columns.description: Schema of the data columns. Units are mandatory — silent unit comparison is forbidden.

uncertainty_column.description: Name of paired error-bar column, if any.

data.description: Row-oriented data. Each row is an object keyed by column name. Original values; no silent transformations.

uncertainty_methodology.description: How error bars were determined. If the source reports uncertainty, document it. If not, explicitly say so — never fake it.

preprocessing_log.description: Every transformation applied (smoothing, normalization, unit conversion). Originals are never modified silently.

original_hash.description: SHA-256 of the source file as uploaded. Reproducibility anchor.

validated.description: Did the Data Engine successfully validate the schema?

validation_warnings.description: Non-fatal issues (e.g. 'sample_rate below recommended for spectral analysis').

validation_errors.description: If non-empty, validated MUST be false.

annotations.description: User-added or imported annotations on the data (e.g. 'avalanche at t=1200s'). Persist into exports.
